name: Visibility + Oceanschedules AWS 2.x Doc Updates
description: Update visibility-aws2x-plan.md and CONFLUENCE-visibility.md now that booking is merged to develop. Create oceanschedules AWS 2.x upgrade plan and confluence design doc.

entries.summary: Created oceanschedules AWS SDK 2.x upgrade plan and Confluence design document
entries.detail:
Created two comprehensive documents for the oceanschedules AWS SDK 2.x migration:

1. **os-aws2x-plan.md** (~650 lines) — Detailed upgrade plan covering:
   - Executive summary with metrics (558 production files, 212 test files, 9 deployable units)
   - 4-phase upgrade approach (POM → API module → process/common → 7 sub-modules)
   - DynamoDB entity migration for RealTimeCache and SchedulesProStaging (flat entities, no nested docs)
   - SQS migration including extended client library replacement
   - S3, SNS, SSM migration details
   - Spark job AWS client lifecycle considerations
   - Unit test plan (212 files), integration test plan (DynamoDB Local)
   - Data format backward compatibility analysis (simpler than booking — epoch seconds only)
   - Risk matrix (SQS extended client, Spark serialization, generic type parameters)
   - Full file change matrix per module

2. **CONFLUENCE-os.md** (~550 lines) — Confluence-style design document matching visibility/docs/CONFLUENCE-visibility.md structure:
   - Requirements, Assumptions, High Level Design with architecture diagrams
   - Low Level Design (DynamoDB table structures, entity migration code, Guice module architecture)
   - Configuration changes (YAML, Java config classes, POM dependencies)
   - Resiliency, backward compatibility, security analysis
   - Unit test plan, review checklist

Key finding: Ocean schedules entities are flat (no @DynamoDBDocument nesting), making the DynamoDB migration simpler than booking/visibility. The main complexity is in the Spark batch pipeline (5 sub-modules) and the SQS extended client library replacement.
